title: Final Capstone: Inventory Operations Launch
summary: Build a two-table makers-market inventory database through six cumulative schema and data-management steps.

sketchBlocks.title: Final Capstone: Inventory Operations Launch
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: In this final capstone project, you will take on the role of a SQL data management specialist tasked with setting up a database for a neighborhood makers market. The goal is to replace a shared spreadsheet with a robust operational database. This project will guide you through creating a schema, loading approved data, applying corrections, and ensuring the database is ready for operational use. By the end of this project, you will have a portfolio-quality three-file launch workspace consisting of `schema.sql`, `seed.sql`, and `query.sql`. Each file will play a crucial role in defining the database structure, loading initial data, and applying necessary changes.

sketchBlocks.title: Step 1: Build the Product Table
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
The first step in setting up the makers market database is to create a table for products. This table will store essential information about each product, including its SKU, name, category, price, status, and any additional notes. The `products` table will have the following columns:

- `id`: INTEGER PRIMARY KEY
- `sku`: TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE
- `name`: TEXT NOT NULL
- `category`: TEXT NOT NULL
- `price`: REAL NOT NULL
- `status`: TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft'
- `notes`: TEXT NULL

This structure ensures that each product has a unique identifier and SKU, mandatory fields for name, category, and price, and optional notes. The status defaults to 'draft' unless specified otherwise.

quizDraft.title: Build the Product Table
quizDraft.prompt: Create the `products` table in `schema.sql` with the specified columns and constraints.
quizDraft.hint: Ensure each column has the correct data type and constraints.

help.concept: Creating a table with specific columns and constraints.
help.hint_1: Use CREATE TABLE with the specified column definitions.
help.hint_2: Remember to set the primary key and unique constraints correctly.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
CREATE TABLE products (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    category TEXT NOT NULL,
    price REAL NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
    notes TEXT NULL
);

solutionFiles.content:
CREATE TABLE products (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    category TEXT NOT NULL,
    price REAL NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
    notes TEXT NULL
);

quizDraft.checkSql: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='products';

quizDraft.title: Build the Stock Event Table
quizDraft.prompt: Create the `stock_events` table in `schema.sql` with the specified columns and constraints, including a foreign key to `products`.
quizDraft.hint: Ensure the foreign key references the correct column in the `products` table.

help.concept: Creating a table with a foreign key constraint.
help.hint_1: Use CREATE TABLE with the specified column definitions and foreign key constraint.
help.hint_2: Ensure the foreign key references the `id` column of the `products` table.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
CREATE TABLE products (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    category TEXT NOT NULL,
    price REAL NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
    notes TEXT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE stock_events (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    product_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    quantity_change INTEGER NOT NULL,
    event_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'receipt',
    note TEXT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(id)
);

quizDraft.checkSql: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_events';

quizDraft.title: Load the Approved Products
quizDraft.prompt: Insert three approved products into the `products` table in `seed.sql`, using explicit column lists and intentional NULLs.
quizDraft.hint: Use explicit column lists and ensure one note is NULL.

help.concept: Inserting data with explicit columns and handling NULL values.
help.hint_1: Use INSERT INTO with explicit column lists.
help.hint_2: Ensure one of the notes is set to NULL.

INSERT INTO products (sku, name, category, price, status, notes) VALUES
    ('SKU001', 'Product A', 'Category 1', 10.99, 'active', NULL),
    ('SKU002', 'Product B', 'Category 2', 15.99, 'active', 'Special edition'),
    ('SKU003', 'Product C', 'Category 3', 12.99, DEFAULT, 'Limited stock');

quizDraft.prompt: Insert three stock events into the `stock_events` table in `seed.sql`, ensuring valid product IDs are used.
quizDraft.hint: Ensure each stock event references a valid product ID.

help.concept: Inserting data with foreign key references.
help.hint_1: Use INSERT INTO with explicit column lists.
help.hint_2: Ensure each product_id matches an existing product.

INSERT INTO products (sku, name, category, price, status, notes) VALUES

    ('SKU003', 'Product C', 'Category 3', 12.99, DEFAULT, 'Limited stock');

INSERT INTO stock_events (product_id, quantity_change, event_type, note) VALUES
    (1, 100, 'receipt', 'Initial stock'),
    (2, 50, 'receipt', 'Restock'),
    (3, 30, 'receipt', 'New shipment');

quizDraft.title: Apply the Launch Correction
quizDraft.prompt: Update the price and status of a specific product in `query.sql` to match the launch brief.
quizDraft.hint: Use the product ID to target the correct row for updating.

help.concept: Updating specific rows using a WHERE clause.
help.hint_1: Use UPDATE with SET to change the price and status.
help.hint_2: Ensure the WHERE clause targets the correct product ID.

quizDraft.starterCode:
-- Update product details
UPDATE products
SET
    -- Set new values here
WHERE
    -- Specify the product ID
;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
UPDATE products
SET
    price = 11.99,
    status = 'active'
WHERE
    id = 1;

quizDraft.title: Remove the Test Event and Finalize
quizDraft.prompt: Delete the test event from `stock_events` in `query.sql` using a narrow WHERE clause.
quizDraft.hint: Identify the test event by its unique note or ID.

help.concept: Deleting specific rows using a WHERE clause.
help.hint_1: Use DELETE FROM with a WHERE clause to target the test event.
help.hint_2: Ensure the WHERE clause is specific to the test event.

quizDraft.starterCode:
-- Delete the test event
DELETE FROM stock_events
WHERE
    -- Specify the condition
;
